calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of 1818 Broadway Condominimum Homeowners' Association'S Petition For Amendment Of Sections 4.4, 4.7 And 6.1 Of The Association'S Declaration Of Covenants, Conditions And Restrictions, Or, In The Alternative, For Reformation Of Section 4.7 Civil Code 1356, 3399-3401
Off Calendar Feb-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. ANN RANKIN, TEL. NO. (510) 655-8886 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. MICHAEL W. FLYNN & JEFFREY G. GIBSON OF GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN & HARRIS, TEL. NO. 673-5600 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS CHRIS & MARYAM NEIL;
ATTY. DAVID P. WASSERMAN OF WASSERMAN-STERN, TEL. NO. 567-9600 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDING PARTY FELIX GROUP LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PETITIONER 1818 BROADWAY CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION'S Petition For Amendment Of Sections 4.4, 4.7 And 6.1 Of The Association'S Declaration Of Covenants, Conditions And Restrictions, Or, In The Alternative, For Reformation Of Section 4.7 Civil Code 1356, 3399-3401 IS GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 6.1, DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 4.4 AND 4.7. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/AL)
